layers.description: Represents natural terrain elevation, ranging from lowland regions to high mountain areas.
Slope median in degrees (°): 0° denotes flat surfaces, while values approaching 90° indicate very steep or cliff-like terrain.
Canopy height (m): larger values represent taller and more developed forests, often indicating higher biomass and denser canopies.

This NASA global night-lights map captures artificial illumination as a proxy for human settlement and economic activity—bright regions signal dense, active populations; dark regions indicate minimal development.
GDP per capita (PPP, USD): low values reflect limited economic resources, whereas high values indicate wealthier districts.
Population count (persons per cell): lower values indicate sparsely populated or uninhabited areas, while higher values represent densely populated regions with more human activity.
Annual average temperature (°C): representing the typical annual thermal conditions at each location.
Annual average precipitation (mm): indicating the typical yearly moisture availability.
